title: Streblus anthropophagorum
taxonomy: Streblus anthropophagorum belongs to the family Moraceae. It is a species of flowering plant in the genus Streblus. The classification of Streblus anthropophagorum is further divided into kingdom Plantae and phylum Magnoliophyta. This plant is native to Brazil and other parts of South America. Streblus anthropophagorum is also known as the Brazilian chestnut or cagaita.
uses: Streblus anthropophagorum is a plant species native to the tropical forests of South America. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions. The plant's bark and leaves are rich in antioxidants and have been reported to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
